qa_note: Kiko's nine standard animations and 16-direction loop preserve the established 3D gecko identity, read correctly at pet size, and pass all deterministic and blind QA gates.

directions.observed: both physical eyes and lifted snout aim upward
directions.reason: vertical up cue is unmistakable at pet size

directions.observed: eyes remain high while snout begins moving screen-right
eyes and head share a clear upper-right aim
directions.reason: quadrant and loop order are clear

directions.observed: head turns further right with a smaller remaining upward cue
nose tip and visible eye aim screen-right
right-facing head begins a controlled downward pitch
eye and snout aim distinctly down-right
directions.reason: quadrant and loop order are clear

directions.observed: deep downward pitch retains a subtle screen-right profile
directions.reason: forms a natural final step before the approved 180 down anchor

directions.observed: both physical eyes and tucked snout aim downward
eyes, snout, and head share a clear lower-left aim
directions.reason: quadrant and loop order are clear

directions.observed: head turns further left with a smaller remaining downward cue
nose tip and visible eye aim screen-left
left-facing head begins a controlled upward pitch
eye and snout aim distinctly up-left
directions.reason: quadrant and loop order are clear

directions.observed: strong upward pitch retains a subtle screen-left profile
directions.reason: forms a natural final step before 000 up

review_warnings: 157.5->180 and 337.5->000 are metric outliers, but normal-size review shows natural foreshortening with planted feet, stable torso and tail, and no conspicuous snap or reversal.
Reported lower-body transparency is intentional separation between feet, legs, and tail rather than an interior body hole.
Legacy standard rows retain Kiko's intentional state-coded hue shifts while preserving material, markings, face, proportions, and silhouette.
